import React, { useState } from 'react'; import { BarChart3, Target, Brain, Zap, TrendingUp, Calculator, Clock, Minimize2, ArrowRight, Activity, MousePointer, Layers, Eye, Cpu, Database, GitBranch, Sparkles, Microscope, Lightbulb, Award, BookOpen, ChevronDown, ChevronRight } from 'lucide-react'; const AnalysisMethodology: React.FC = () => { const [activeTab, setActiveTab] = useState<'methodology' | 'metrics' | 'definitions' | 'science'>('methodology'); const [selectedMetric, setSelectedMetric] = useState(null); const metricCategories = { ergonomic: { title: 'Ergonomic Factors', color: 'text-green-400', icon: , metrics: [ { name: 'effort', title: 'Total Effort', description: 'Cumulative typing strain based on key difficulty and finger strength', formula: 'Σ(row_difficulty × column_difficulty × character_frequency)', insight: 'Lower effort reduces fatigue and increases typing endurance', goodRange: '< 1.15', impact: 'high' }, { name: 'pinky_distance', title: 'Pinky Travel Distance', description: 'Proportion of total finger travel performed by weakest fingers', formula: 'pinky_movement / total_movement', insight: 'Minimizing pinky usage prevents RSI and improves comfort', goodRange: '< 15%', impact: 'high' }, { name: 'lateral_stretch_pct', title: 'Lateral Stretches', description: 'Awkward sideways finger stretches within same hand', formula: 'lateral_stretch_bigrams / total_bigrams × 100', insight: 'Reduces finger strain and prevents uncomfortable movements', goodRange: '< 5%', impact: 'medium' } ] }, flow: { title: 'Flow & Rhythm', color: 'text-blue-400', icon: , metrics: [ { name: 'roll_in_pct', title: 'Inward Rolls', description: 'Smooth finger sequences rolling toward keyboard center', formula: 'inward_roll_bigrams / total_bigrams × 100', insight: 'Natural rolling motions increase typing speed and feel', goodRange: '> 15%', impact: 'high' }, { name: 'trigram_alt_pct', title: 'Hand Alternation', description: 'Three-letter sequences alternating between hands', formula: 'alternating_trigrams / total_trigrams × 100', insight: 'Hand alternation allows one hand to rest while other types', goodRange: '> 60%', impact: 'medium' }, { name: 'tri_redirect_pct', title: 'Direction Changes', description: 'Trigrams requiring awkward direction reversals', formula: 'redirect_trigrams / total_trigrams × 100', insight: 'Fewer direction changes create smoother typing flow', goodRange: '< 20%', impact: 'medium' } ] }, precision: { title: 'Precision & Accuracy', color: 'text-purple-400', icon: , metrics: [ { name: 'same_finger_bigrams_pct', title: 'Same Finger Bigrams', description: 'Letter pairs requiring same finger consecutively', formula: 'same_finger_bigrams / total_bigrams × 100', insight: 'Same finger usage slows typing and increases errors', goodRange: '< 2%', impact: 'high' }, { name: 'pinky_scissors_pct', title: 'Pinky Scissors', description: 'Awkward pinky-ring finger crossing movements', formula: 'scissor_bigrams / total_bigrams × 100', insight: 'Scissoring motions are unnatural and error-prone', goodRange: '< 0.5%', impact: 'high' }, { name: 'skip_bigrams_pct', title: 'Skip Bigrams', description: 'Adjacent keys skipped in same row sequence', formula: 'skip_bigrams / total_bigrams × 100', insight: 'Skipping keys can lead to misfires and typos', goodRange: '< 3%', impact: 'medium' } ] }, position: { title: 'Key Positioning', color: 'text-yellow-400', icon: , metrics: [ { name: 'col5_6_pct', title: 'Center Column Usage', description: 'Usage of hard-to-reach center columns (T, Y keys)', formula: 'center_keystrokes / total_keystrokes × 100', insight: 'Center columns are harder to reach and less accurate', goodRange: '< 8%', impact: 'medium' }, { name: 'pinky_off_home_pct', title: 'Pinky Off-Home', description: 'Pinky keystrokes away from home row position', formula: 'pinky_off_home / pinky_keystrokes × 100', insight: 'Keeping pinkies on home row improves accuracy', goodRange: '< 30%', impact: 'medium' }, { name: 'two_row_jumps_pct', title: 'Row Jumping', description: 'Bigrams requiring two-row vertical movements', formula: 'two_row_jumps / total_bigrams × 100', insight: 'Large vertical movements slow typing and cause errors', goodRange: '< 8%', impact: 'medium' } ] } }; return (

Scientific Analysis Framework

Discover how I analyze keyboard layouts using advanced computational linguistics, biomechanical modeling, and statistical analysis to optimize your typing experience.

{/* Navigation Tabs */}
{[ { id: 'methodology', label: 'Methodology', icon: }, { id: 'metrics', label: 'Metrics Deep Dive', icon: }, { id: 'definitions', label: 'Metric Definitions', icon: }, { id: 'science', label: 'The Science', icon: } ].map(tab => ( ))}
{/* Tab Content */} {activeTab === 'methodology' && (
{/* Overview */}

Analysis Overview

My analysis engine processes keyboard layouts using a methodology inspired by the renowned cyanophage analyzer, evaluating 17 key metrics that impact typing comfort, speed, and accuracy.

17 Core Metrics

Comprehensive analysis of typing patterns and biomechanics

Real-world Data

Analysis based on actual text corpora and word frequencies

Biomechanical Focus

Considers hand anatomy, finger strength, and ergonomics

{/* Process Steps */}

Analysis Process

1

Text Corpus Analysis

Process large text samples to determine letter, bigram, and trigram frequencies. I analyze text from accessible sources suitable for a student project.

Data Sources:

  • • Project Gutenberg - Public domain books for text analysis
  • • Kaggle English Corpora - Curated text datasets for students
  • • English Words GitHub - Word frequency lists and corpora
2

Layout Mapping

Map each character to its physical position and assigned finger, considering standard touch typing technique and hand positions.

3

Metric Calculation

Calculate all 17 metrics using frequency-weighted analysis, biomechanical models, and statistical methods validated by research.

4

Normalization & Scoring

Normalize metrics across different text types and generate personalized recommendations based on user profiles and preferences.

)} {/* Metrics Deep Dive Tab */} {activeTab === 'metrics' && (
{Object.entries(metricCategories).map(([categoryKey, category]) => (
{category.icon}

{category.title}

{category.metrics.map((metric) => (
setSelectedMetric(selectedMetric === metric.name ? null : metric.name)} >

{metric.title}

{metric.impact} impact

{metric.description}

Target: {metric.goodRange}
{selectedMetric === metric.name && (

Formula

{metric.formula}

Why It Matters

{metric.insight}

)}
))}
))}
)} {/* Metric Definitions Tab */} {activeTab === 'definitions' && (

Complete Metric Definitions

All 17 metrics used in keyboard layout analysis, with exact formulas, implementation details, and optimal target ranges based on ergonomic research and real-world typing data.

{/* Physical Effort Metrics */}

Physical Effort & Distance

Effort

Total physical strain required to type text, accounting for key difficulty and character frequency.

Formula:

effort = totalEffort / totalChars where totalEffort = Σ(rowDifficulty × colDifficulty) for each character in corpus

Difficulty Multipliers:

  • • Rows: Top=1.3x, Home=1.0x, Bottom=1.2x
  • • Columns: Center (4,5)=1.4x, Others=1.0-1.1x
  • • Target: < 1.15 (excellent), < 1.25 (good)

Distance

Average Euclidean distance fingers travel between consecutive keystrokes.

Formula:

distance = totalDistance / totalTransitions totalDistance = Σ√((x₂-x₁)² + (y₂-y₁)²)

Implementation:

  • • Uses physical key coordinates (row, column)
  • • Tracks previous position for each character
  • • Target: < 1.6 (excellent), < 2.0 (good)

Pinky Distance

Proportion of total finger travel performed by the weakest fingers (pinkies).

Formula:

pinky_distance = pinkyDistance / totalDistance where pinkyDistance includes LP and RP movements

Details:

  • • Only counts when finger = 'LP' or 'RP'
  • • Critical for RSI prevention
  • • Target: < 0.15 (15%)

Pinky Off-Home %

Percentage of pinky keystrokes that are not on the home row position.

Formula:

pinky_off_home_pct = (pinkyOffHome / pinkyKeystrokes) × 100 where off-home = position ≠ homePosition[finger]

Home Positions:

  • • LP: [1,0] (A key), RP: [1,9] (; key)
  • • Checks both row and column position
  • • Target: < 30%
{/* Bigram Analysis Metrics */}

Bigram Movement Analysis

Same Finger Bigrams %

Percentage of letter pairs requiring the same finger consecutively.

Formula:

sfb_pct = (sameFingerBigrams / totalBigrams) × 100 where finger1 === finger2 for bigram

Impact:

  • • Severely slows typing speed
  • • Increases error rate significantly
  • • Target: < 2% (modern layouts: < 1%)

Skip Bigrams %

Movements that skip one key on the same row (2-key gap).

Formula:

skip_pct = (skipBigrams / totalBigrams) × 100 where pos1[0] === pos2[0] && |pos1[1] - pos2[1]| === 2
  • • Same row requirement
  • • Skip exactly one key between
  • • Target: < 3%

Skip Bigrams 2u %

Movements that skip two keys on the same row (3-key gap).

Formula:

skip2_pct = (skipBigrams2 / totalBigrams) × 100 where pos1[0] === pos2[0] && |pos1[1] - pos2[1]| === 3
  • • Even more awkward than regular skips
  • • Causes significant hand contortion
  • • Target: < 1%

Lateral Stretch %

Uncomfortable stretching motions within the same hand.

Formula:

lateral_pct = (lateralStretch / totalBigrams) × 100 where sameHand && differentFingers && |pos1[1] - pos2[1]| > 2
  • • Same hand, different fingers required
  • • Column distance > 2 triggers stretch
  • • Target: < 5%

Pinky Scissors %

Awkward pinky-ring finger crossing movements.

Formula:

scissors_pct = (pinkyScissors / totalBigrams) × 100 where isPinkyRing && sameHand && crossing

Detection Logic:

  • • Pinky-Ring or Ring-Pinky combinations
  • • Same hand movement
  • • Crossing pattern detected
  • • Target: < 0.5%

Two Row Jumps %

Bigrams requiring 2+ row vertical movements (any finger).

Formula:

two_row_pct = (twoRowJumps / totalBigrams) × 100 where |pos1[0] - pos2[0]| >= 2
  • • Large vertical movements
  • • Slows typing and increases errors
  • • Target: < 8%
{/* Flow and Rhythm Metrics */}

Flow & Rhythm Analysis

Inward Rolls %

Natural finger movements rolling toward keyboard center.

Formula:

roll_in_pct = (rollInBigrams / totalBigrams) × 100 where sameHand && sameRow && rollingInward

Direction Logic:

  • • Left hand: column increases (left→right)
  • • Right hand: column decreases (right→left)
  • • Target: > 15% (higher is better)

Outward Rolls %

Finger movements rolling away from keyboard center.

Formula:

roll_out_pct = (rollOutBigrams / totalBigrams) × 100 where sameHand && sameRow && rollingOutward

Direction Logic:

  • • Left hand: column decreases (right→left)
  • • Right hand: column increases (left→right)
  • • Target: Moderate levels (5-15%)

Trigram Alternation %

Three-letter sequences alternating between hands (L-R-L or R-L-R).

Formula:

tri_alt_pct = (altTrigrams / totalTrigrams) × 100 where hand1 ≠ hand2 && hand2 ≠ hand3
  • • Allows one hand to rest while other types
  • • Enables very fast typing speeds
  • • Target: > 60%

Trigram Redirects %

Three-letter patterns requiring awkward direction changes.

Formula:

redirect_pct = (redirectTrigrams / totalTrigrams) × 100 where (dir1 > 0 && dir2 < 0) || (dir1 < 0 && dir2 > 0)

Direction Detection:

  • • dir1 = pos2[1] - pos1[1] (horizontal movement)
  • • dir2 = pos3[1] - pos2[1] (next movement)
  • • Redirect = direction sign change
  • • Target: < 20%
{/* Position and Usage Metrics */}

Key Position & Usage

Column 5,6 Usage %

Usage percentage of the center columns (T/Y and G/H positions in QWERTY).

Formula:

col56_pct = (col56Keystrokes / totalKeystrokes) × 100 where pos[1] === 4 || pos[1] === 5 (0-indexed)

Details:

  • • Columns 5 & 6 are harder to reach accurately
  • • Higher usage may indicate suboptimal layout
  • • Context matters: what letters are placed there
  • • Target: < 8% (context dependent)

Two Row SFB %

Same finger bigrams that also require 2+ row jumps (worst case scenario).

Formula:

two_row_sfb_pct = (twoRowSfb / totalSfb) × 100 where finger1 === finger2 && |pos1[0] - pos2[0]| >= 2

Critical Insight:

  • • Combines worst aspects of SFB and row jumping
  • • Calculated as percentage of existing SFBs
  • • Extremely detrimental to speed and comfort
  • • Target: < 10% of total SFBs
{/* Implementation Constants */}

Implementation Constants

Key Difficulty Multipliers

Row Penalties:

{'ROW_DIFFICULTY = {'} 0: 1.3, // Top row 1: 1.0, // Home row 2: 1.2 // Bottom row {'}'}

Column Penalties:

{'COLUMN_DIFFICULTY = {'} 0,1,2,3,7,8,9: 1.0-1.1, 4,5: 1.4 // Center columns {'}'}

Finger Assignments

Standard Touch Typing:

{'FINGER_MAP = {'} // Left hand 'q,a,z': 'LP', // Left Pinky 'w,s,x': 'LR', // Left Ring 'e,d,c': 'LM', // Left Middle 'r,f,v,t,g,b': 'LI', // Left Index // Right hand (mirror) {'}'}

Home Row Positions:

{'HOME_POSITIONS = {'} 'LP': [1,0], 'LR': [1,1], ... {'}'}
)} {/* The Science Tab */} {activeTab === 'science' && (

Research Foundation

Corpus Linguistics

Our analysis is grounded in computational linguistics, using massive text corpora to determine real-world character and sequence frequencies:

  • • 1M+ words from English literature and articles
  • • 500K+ words from programming code repositories
  • • Statistical validation across multiple text types
  • • Dynamic weighting based on user context

Biomechanical Modeling

We incorporate established research on hand anatomy and ergonomics:

  • • Finger strength ratios based on biomechanical studies
  • • Range of motion limitations for each finger
  • • Fatigue models for repetitive strain prediction
  • • Hand posture considerations for natural positioning

Mathematical Framework

Frequency-Weighted Analysis

Every metric is calculated using statistical weighting based on real text frequencies:

metric_score = Σ(frequency[pattern] × difficulty[pattern]) / Σ(frequency[pattern])

Bigram Analysis

676 possible letter pairs analyzed for movement patterns

Trigram Analysis

17,576 three-letter patterns for flow and alternation

Position Analysis

30-key layout with finger assignments and reach distances

)} {/* Footer */}

Open Source & Research Based

Our analysis methodology is based on the open-source cyanophage analyzer and academic research in ergonomics and biomechanics. All calculations are transparent and can be verified through our source code.

MIT Licensed Research Validated Community Driven
); }; export default AnalysisMethodology;
